title: cerastium varians subsp. pumilum
intro: Cerastium varians subsp. pumilum, also known as the alpine chickweed or dwarf chickweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of cerastium varians subsp. pumilum include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected area.
